_meta.note: Worked example, written 2026-04-17 against an already-shipped fix (commit at index.html:1567). Used to pressure-test the dossier schema before wiring the gate into /autopilot.

ac_verbatim: After Settings > Clear Data is confirmed, no `meal:*` keys remain in localStorage (only `pin` may persist).
After Settings > Clear Data is confirmed, the home meal card no longer renders a fasting suggestion derived from prior meal state — it returns to the empty/initial state.
Confirm prompt copy unchanged unless the ticket says otherwise.
ac_paraphrase_check: Verbatim quoted above; no paraphrase used in scope-gate or implementation reasoning. Any restatement (e.g. 'clear all per-run state') is annotation, not the AC.

scope_gate.rule: Label is Bug or Improvement, not Feature
No touch to docs/PRINCIPLES.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, sw.js, manifest.json, cache version, or service-worker registration path
scope_gate.evidence_cmd: Planned diff = index.html clearData() body only; no other files in files_to_touch

scope_gate.rule: No localStorage shape change; no new tracked field added
scope_gate.evidence_cmd: Fix removes additional keys at clear-time; does not add or alter any key written elsewhere. grep 'meal:last' index.html -> only setter and getter sites; sweep removes both.
scope_gate.result: shape unchanged; deletion list expanded only
scope_gate.judgement_note: Borderline: a 'wider sweep' could plausibly delete a key another caller relies on. Confirmed by enumerating writers (see adjacent_surfaces). All non-PIN writers expect their data to be wiped by Clear Data — that is the feature's contract.

scope_gate.evidence_cmd: Ticket includes a Suggested Fix block with a concrete code sample; AC is fully specified.

scope_gate.rule: All ACs are code-verifiable (diff + grep + browser render)
scope_gate.evidence_cmd: AC#1 = Object.keys(localStorage) assertion in browser; AC#2 = DOM snapshot of meal card after clear; AC#3 = grep confirm()

scope_gate.rule: Fits one allowed change shape (copy / CSS-no-reflow / a11y-attribute / isolated bugfix with single root cause)
scope_gate.evidence_cmd: Single function (clearData), single root cause (key allowlist too narrow), single code site (index.html:1565-1571)

all_call_sites: all writers and readers in the meal-logging flow
change_required_at: none — fix removes via sweep, not by name
this is the new code site

adjacent_surfaces.string_or_symbol: future key risk: draft:episode (named in ticket)
adjacent_surfaces.note: Sweep approach is forward-compatible with this future key. Recorded for transparency, not a current scope item.

root_cause_one_sentence: clearData() iterated localStorage keys but only removed those starting with 'entry:', leaving meal:* (and any other future non-PIN keys) orphaned.
approach_alternatives_considered: Explicit removeItem('meal:last') + removeItem('meal:last_drink') — rejected: same bug pattern (allowlist), will rot when next key is added (e.g. draft:episode).
Prefix-based sweep removing entry:* + meal:* — rejected: same fragility, narrower benefit.
premortem_embarrassment: If this autopilot run ships and the user reports it: most likely failure is an unintentionally-deleted key the user values. Specifically, if a future caller adds e.g. `prefs:theme` and assumes it survives Clear Data, the sweep will silently wipe it. Acceptable for v1 of the patient app (PIN is the only non-clearable contract today, per the ticket's own framing), but worth flagging in the Linear comment so the user knows the contract was implicitly broadened.

steps: Return to home, assert meal card shows 'Tap when you eat...' empty state, not a fasting suggestion.

confidence_justification: Single function, single root cause, ticket includes a working code sample, fix matches ticket's preferred approach. Confidence not 100 because the implicit contract change (sweep vs. allowlist) carries non-zero future risk that is not covered by current ACs.
